channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: Cyber valuations climb as capital concentrates, AI security expands
item.description: Venture funding in cybersecurity continued to concentrate in large private rounds at the end of 2025, driving valuations higher across stages. Data from DataTribe shows total capital invested approached $150 billion f...

item.title: Microsoft adds domain libraries and Copilot integration to the quantum development kit
item.description: The Microsoft Quantum Development Kit (QDK) is an open-source toolkit that runs on laptops and in common development environments. It includes code, simulators, libraries, and workflows that work with Visual Studio Co...

item.title: Airline brands become launchpads for phishing, crypto fraud
item.description: Airline brands sit at the center of peak travel booking cycles, loyalty programs, and high value transactions. Criminal groups continue to register thousands of lookalike domains tied to these brands, targeting travel...

item.title: CISA Confirms Active Exploitation of FileZen CVE-2026-25108 Vulnerability
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a recently disclosed vulnerability in FileZen to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitat...

item.title: Edge systems take the brunt of internet-wide exploitation attempts
item.description: Internet-facing VPNs, routers, and remote access services absorbed sustained exploitation attempts throughout the second half of 2025, with nearly 3 billion malicious sessions recorded over 162 days. The concentration...

item.title: Phishing campaign targets freight and logistics orgs in the US, Europe
item.description: A financially motivated threat group dubbed "Diesel Vortex" is stealing credentials from freight and logistics operators in the U.S. and Europe in phishing attacks using 52 domains. [...]

item.title: Wynn Resorts confirms employee data breach after extortion threat
item.description: Wynn Resorts has confirmed that a hacker stole employee data from its systems after the company was listed on the ShinyHunters extortion gang's data leak site. [...]

item.title: 1Campaign platform helps malicious Google ads evade detection
item.description: A newly identified cybercrime service known as 1Campaign is enabling threat actors to run malicious Google Ads that remain online for extended periods while evading scrutiny from security researchers. [...]

item.title: Attackers Now Need Just 29 Minutes to Own a Network
item.description: Credential misuse, AI tools, and security blind spots help attackers move through breached networks faster than ever, CrowdStrike finds.

item.title: Lazarus Group Picks a New Poison: Medusa Ransomware
item.description: The North Korean threat group also leveraged Comebacker backdoor, Blindingcan RAT, and info stealer Infohook in its recent attacks.

item.title: CarGurus data breach exposes information of 12.4 million accounts
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion group has published personal information in more than 12 million records allegedly stolen from CarGurus, a U.S.-based digital auto platform. [...]

item.title: Open Redirects: A Forgotten Vulnerability?, (Tue, Feb 24th)
item.description: In 2010, OWASP added "Unvalidated Redirects and Forwards" to its Top 10 list and merged it into "Sensitive Data Exposure" in 2013 [owasp1] [owasp2]. Open redirects are often overlooked, and their impact is not always...

item.title: Microsoft adds Copilot data controls to all storage locations
item.description: Microsoft is expanding data loss prevention (DLP) controls to block the Microsoft 365 Copilot AI assistant from processing confidential Word, Excel, and PowerPoint documents, regardless of their location. [...]

item.title: Cost of Insider Incidents Surges 20% to Nearly $20m
item.description: DTEX claims insider incidents cost $19.5m in 2025, with employee negligence most expensive

item.title: Multifaceted Phishing Scheme Deceives Bitpanda Customers
item.description: Phishing attack mimicking Bitpanda targets users, harvesting credentials and personal information

item.title: UAC-0050 Targets European Financial Institution With Spoofed Domain and RMS Malware
item.description: A Russia-aligned threat actor has been observed targeting a European financial institution as part of a social engineering attack to likely facilitate intelligence gathering or financial theft, signaling a possible ex...

item.title: GitHub Issues Abused in Copilot Attack Leading to Repository Takeover
item.description: Attackers can inject malicious instructions in a GitHub Issue that are automatically processed by Copilot when launching a Codespace from that issue. The post GitHub Issues Abused in Copilot Attack Leading to Reposito...

item.title: Is AI Good for Democracy?
item.description: Politicians fixate on the global race for technological supremacy between US and China. They debate geopolitical implications of chip exports, latest model releases from each country, and military applications of AI....

item.title: Taiwan Security Firm Confirms Flaw Flagged by CISA Likely Exploited by Chinese APTs
item.description: The vulnerability in TeamT5 ThreatSonar Anti-Ransomware was recently added to CISA’s KEV catalog. The post Taiwan Security Firm Confirms Flaw Flagged by CISA Likely Exploited by Chinese APTs appeared first on Security...

item.title: Identity Prioritization isn't a Backlog Problem - It's a Risk Math Problem
item.description: Most identity programs still prioritize work the way they prioritize IT tickets: by volume, loudness, or “what failed a control check.” That approach breaks the moment your environment stops being mostly-human and mos...

item.title: Lazarus Group Uses Medusa Ransomware in Middle East and U.S. Healthcare Attacks
item.description: The North Korea-linked Lazarus Group (aka Diamond Sleet and Pompilus) has been observed using Medusa ransomware in an attack targeting an unnamed entity in the Middle East, according to a new report by the Symantec an...

item.title: ShinyHunters extortion gang claims Odido breach affecting millions
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang has claimed responsibility for breaching Dutch telecommunications provider Odido and stealing millions of user records from its compromised systems. [...]

item.title: North Korean Lazarus group linked to Medusa ransomware attacks
item.description: North Korean state-backed hackers associated with the Lazarus threat group are targeting U.S. healthcare organizations in extortion attacks using the Medusa ransomware. [...]

item.title: Arkanix Stealer: AI-assisted info-stealer shuts down after brief campaign
item.description: Arkanix Stealer surfaced in late 2025 as a short-lived info-stealer, likely built as an AI-assisted experiment and quickly abandoned. Arkanix Stealer emerged in late 2025 as a short-lived information-stealing malware...

item.title: Anonymous Fénix Members Arrested in Spain
item.description: The group’s administrator and moderator were arrested last year, and two other members were arrested this month. The post Anonymous Fénix Members Arrested in Spain appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Android mental health apps with 14.7M installs filled with security flaws
item.description: Several mental health mobile apps with millions of downloads on Google Play contain security vulnerabilities that could expose users' sensitive medical information. [...]

item.title: More Than Dashboards: AI Decisions Must Be Provable
item.description: AI systems have to be able to show a record of what happened and how.

item.title: 600+ FortiGate Devices Hacked by AI-Armed Amateur
item.description: A Russian-speaking hacker used generative AI to compromise the FortiGate firewalls, targeting credentials and backups for possible follow-on ransomware attacks.

item.title: Ad tech firm Optimizely confirms data breach after vishing attack
item.description: New York-based ad tech company Optimizely has notified an undisclosed number of customers of a data breach after threat actors compromised some of its systems in a voice phishing attack. [...]

item.title: Wormable XMRig Campaign Uses BYOVD Exploit and Time-Based Logic Bomb
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new cryptojacking campaign that uses pirated software bundles as lures to deploy a bespoke XMRig miner program on compromised hosts. "Analysis of the recovered dro...

item.title: Autonomous AI Agents Provide New Class of Supply Chain Attack
item.description: While this campaign targets crypto wallets and steals money, the methodology has far wider potential that could be used by other attackers. The post Autonomous AI Agents Provide New Class of Supply Chain Attack appear...

item.title: CVE-2026-1731 fuels ongoing attacks on BeyondTrust remote access products
item.description: Attackers are exploiting CVE-2026-1731 in BeyondTrust RS and PRA to deploy VShell, gain persistence, move laterally, and control compromised systems. Threat actors are actively exploiting a recently disclosed critical...

item.title: On the Security of Password Managers
item.description: Good article on password managers that secretly have a backdoor. New research shows that these claims aren’t true in all cases, particularly when account recovery is in place or password managers are set to share vaul...

item.title: How Exposed Endpoints Increase Risk Across LLM Infrastructure
item.description: As more organizations run their own Large Language Models (LLMs), they are also deploying more internal services and Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) to support those models. Modern security risks are being i...

item.title: AI-powered campaign compromises 600 FortiGate systems worldwide
item.description: A Russian-speaking cybercriminal used commercial generative AI tools to hack over 600 FortiGate devices across 55 countries. Amazon Threat Intelligence reports that a Russian-speaking, financially motivated threat act...

item.title: Malicious npm Packages Harvest Crypto Keys, CI Secrets, and API Tokens
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed what they say is an active "Shai-Hulud-like" supply chain worm campaign that has leveraged a cluster of at least 19 malicious npm packages to enable credential harvesting and c...

item.title: Anthropic unveils Claude Code Security to detect and fix code bugs
item.description: Anthropic launches Claude Code Security, an AI tool that scans code for vulnerabilities and suggests how to address them. Anthropic has introduced Claude Code Security, a new AI-powered service designed to scan softwa...

item.title: Japanese-Language Phishing Emails, (Sat, Feb 21st)
item.description: Introduction

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Cartoon
item.description: I like this one . As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: Attackers Use New Tool to Scan for React2Shell Exposure
item.description: Researchers say threat actors wielded the sophisticated — and unfortunately named — toolkit to target high-value networks for React2Shell exploitation.

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-a...

item.title: 'God-Like' Attack Machines: AI Agents Ignore Security Policies
item.description: Microsoft Copilot recently summarized and leaked user emails; but any AI agent will go above and beyond to complete assigned tasks, even breaking through their carefully designed guardrails.

item.title: Lessons From AI Hacking: Every Model, Every Layer Is Risky
item.description: After two years of finding flaws in AI infrastructure, two Wiz researchers advise security pros to worry less about prompt injection and more about vulnerabilities.

item.title: BeyondTrust Flaw Used for Web Shells, Backdoors, and Data Exfiltration
item.description: Threat actors have been observed exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting BeyondTrust Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) products to conduct a wide range of malicious action...

item.title: Identity Cyber Scores: The New Metric Shaping Cyber Insurance in 2026
item.description: With one in three cyber-attacks now involving compromised employee accounts, insurers and regulators are placing far greater emphasis on identity posture when assessing cyber risk. For many organizations, however, the...

item.title: Ukrainian National Sentenced to 5 Years in North Korea IT Worker Fraud Case
item.description: A 29-year-old Ukrainian national has been sentenced to five years in prison in the U.S. for his role in facilitating North Korea's fraudulent information technology (IT) worker scheme. In November 2025, Oleksandr "Ale...

item.title: VShell and SparkRAT Observed in Exploitation of BeyondTrust Critical Vulnerability (CVE-2026-1731)
item.description: CVE-2026-1731 is an RCE vulnerability in identity platform BeyondTrust. This flaw allows attackers control of systems without login credentials. The post VShell and SparkRAT Observed in Exploitation of BeyondTrust Cri...

item.title: Supply Chain Attack Secretly Installs OpenClaw for Cline Users
item.description: The malicious version of Cline's npm package — 2.3.0 — was downloaded more than 4,000 times before it was removed.

item.title: Best-in-Class 'Starkiller' Phishing Kit Bypasses MFA
item.description: A user-friendly PhaaS tool beats standard methods for detecting phishing attacks by live-proxying legitimate login sites.

item.title: Malicious AI
item.description: Interesting : Summary: An AI agent of unknown ownership autonomously wrote and published a personalized hit piece about me after I rejected its code, attempting to damage my reputation and shame me into accepting its...

item.title: From Exposure to Exploitation: How AI Collapses Your Response Window
item.description: We’ve all seen this before: a developer deploys a new cloud workload and grants overly broad permissions just to keep the sprint moving. An engineer generates a "temporary" API key for testing and forgets to revoke it...

item.title: Fake IPTV Apps Spread Massiv Android Malware Targeting Mobile Banking Users
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Android trojan called Massiv that's designed to facilitate device takeover (DTO) attacks for financial theft. The malware, according to ThreatFabric, masquerad...

item.title: Scam Abuses Gemini Chatbots to Convince People to Buy Fake Crypto
item.description: A convincing presale site for phony "Google Coin" features an AI assistant that engages victims with a slick sales pitch, funneling payment to attackers.

item.title: Critical Grandstream VoIP Bug Highlights SMB Security Blind Spot
item.description: CVE-2026-2329 allows unauthenticated root-level access to SMB phone infrastructure, so attackers can intercept calls, commit toll fraud, and impersonate users.

item.title: Grandstream GXP1600 VoIP Phones Exposed to Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security flaw in the Grandstream GXP1600 series of VoIP phones that could allow an attacker to seize control of susceptible devices. The vulnerability, tracked as CV...

item.title: AI Found Twelve New Vulnerabilities in OpenSSL
item.description: The title of the post is” What AI Security Research Looks Like When It Works ,” and I agree: In the latest OpenSSL security release> on January 27, 2026, twelve new zero-day vulnerabilities (meaning unknown to the mai...

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2021-22175 GitLab Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability CVE-2026-2...

item.title: Dell RecoverPoint for VMs Zero-Day CVE-2026-22769 Exploited Since Mid-2024
item.description: A maximum severity security vulnerability in Dell RecoverPoint for Virtual Machines has been exploited as a zero-day by a suspected China-nexus threat cluster dubbed UNC6201 since mid-2024, according to a new report f...

item.title: 3 Ways to Start Your Intelligent Workflow Program
item.description: Security, IT, and engineering teams today are under relentless pressure to accelerate outcomes, cut operational drag, and unlock the full potential of AI and automation. But simply investing in tools isn’t enough. 88%...

item.title: Introducing "AI Unlocked: Decoding Prompt Injection," a New Interactive Challenge
Exposing Insider Threats through Data Protection, Identity, and HR Context
Supply Chain Attack Embeds Malware in Android Devices
item.description: Keenadu downloads payloads that hijack browser searches, commit ad fraud, and execute other actions without user knowledge.

item.title: RMM Abuse Explodes as Hackers Ditch Malware
item.description: Remote monitoring and management (RMM) software offers hackers multiple benefits, including stealth, persistence, and operational efficiency.

item.title: ClickFix Attacks Abuses DNS Lookup Command to Deliver ModeloRAT
item.description: ClickFix campaigns have adapted to the latest defenses with a new technique to trick users into infecting their own machines with malware.

item.title: Critical Vulnerabilities in Ivanti EPMM Exploited
item.description: We discuss widespread exploitation of Ivanti EPMM zero-day vulns CVE-2026-1281 and CVE-2026-1340. Attackers are deploying web shells and backdoors. The post Critical Vulnerabilities in Ivanti EPMM Exploited appeared f...

item.title: Researchers Show Copilot and Grok Can Be Abused as Malware C2 Proxies
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed that artificial intelligence (AI) assistants that support web browsing or URL fetching capabilities can be turned into stealthy command-and-control (C2) relays, a technique tha...

item.title: Keenadu Firmware Backdoor Infects Android Tablets via Signed OTA Updates
item.description: A new Android backdoor that's embedded deep into the device firmware can silently harvest data and remotely control its behavior, according to new findings from Kaspersky. The Russian cybersecurity vendor said it disc...

item.title: SmartLoader Attack Uses Trojanized Oura MCP Server to Deploy StealC Infostealer
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new SmartLoader campaign that involves distributing a trojanized version of a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server associated with Oura Health to deliver an informa...

item.title: CISA Adds Four Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added four new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2008-0015 Microsoft Windows Video ActiveX Control Remote Code Execution Vuln...

item.title: The Promptware Kill Chain
item.description: Attacks against modern generative artificial intelligence (AI) large language models (LLMs) pose a real threat. Yet discussions around these attacks and their potential defenses are dangerously myopic. The dominant na...

item.title: Safe and Inclusive E‑Society: How Lithuania Is Bracing for AI‑Driven Cyber Fraud
item.description: Technologies are evolving fast, reshaping economies, governance, and daily life. Yet, as innovation accelerates, so do digital risks. Technological change is no longer abstract for such a country as Lithuania, as well...

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.description: This is a current list of where and when I am scheduled to speak: I’m speaking at Ontario Tech University in Oshawa, Ontario, Canada, at 2 PM ET on Thursday, February 26, 2026. I’m speaking at the Personal AI Summit i...

item.title: Phishing on the Edge of the Web and Mobile Using QR Codes
item.description: We discuss the extensive use of malicious QR codes using URL shorteners, in-app deep links and direct APK downloads to bypass mobile security. The post Phishing on the Edge of the Web and Mobile Using QR Codes appeare...

item.title: Zscaler-SquareX Deal Boosts Zero Trust, Secure Browsing Capabilities
item.description: Zscaler's acquisition of SquareX comes as competitors like CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks also invest in secure browser technologies.

item.title: Microsoft Under Pressure to Bolster Defenses for BYOVD Attacks
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting security gaps to weaponize Windows drivers and terminate security processes in targeted networks, and there may be no easy fixes in sight.

item.title: AI Agents 'Swarm,' Security Complexity Follows Suit
item.description: As AI deployments scale and start to include packs of agents autonomously working in concert, organizations face a naturally amplified attack surface.

item.title: Shedding old code with ecdysis: graceful restarts for Rust services at Cloudflare
item.description: ecdysis is a Rust library enabling zero-downtime upgrades for network services. After five years protecting millions of connections at Cloudflare, it’s now open source.

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-1731 BeyondTrust Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) OS...

item.title: Kimwolf Botnet Swamps Anonymity Network I2P
item.description: For the past week, the massive "Internet of Things" (IoT) botnet known as Kimwolf has been disrupting the The Invisible Internet Project (I2P), a decentralized, encrypted communications network designed to anonymize a...

item.title: Top Cyber Industry Defenses Spike CO2 Emissions
item.description: Organizations can improve their climate footprints by optimizing two specific cybersecurity protections, without incurring added risks.

item.title: US Court Hands Crypto Scammer 20 Years in $73m Case
item.description: A federal court has sentenced crypto-scammer Daren Li to 20 years in absentia

item.title: FIRST Forecasts Record-Breaking 50,000+ CVEs in 2026
item.description: This year should break all the records in terms of vulnerability disclosed, reaching or even surpassing 50,000 new CVEs disclosed

item.title: Microsoft Fixes Six Zero Day Vulnerability in February Patch Tuesday
item.description: Six actively exploited zero-day bug have been patched by Microsoft

item.title: Patch Tuesday, February 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft today released updates to fix more than 50 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, including patches for a whopping six "zero-day" vulnerabilities that attackers are already explo...

item.title: In Bypassing MFA, ZeroDayRAT Is 'Textbook Stalkerware'
item.description: With access to SIM, location data, and a preview of recent SMSes, attackers have everything they need for account takeover or targeted social engineering.

item.title: Microsoft Patches 6 Actively Exploited Zero-Days
item.description: Three of those zero-days are security feature bypass flaws, which give attackers a way to slip past built-in protections in multiple Microsoft products.

item.title: OT Attacks Get Scary With 'Living-off-the-Plant' Techniques
item.description: Ironically, security by obscurity has helped prevent dangerous OT attacks in recent years. It won't be that way forever.

item.title: Phorpiex Phishing Delivers Low-Noise Global Group Ransomware
item.description: High-volume phishing campaign delivers Phorpiex malware via malicious Windows Shortcut files

item.title: “Digital Parasite” Warning as Attackers Favor Stealth for Extortion
item.description: Picus Security warns of the increasingly sophisticated ways malicious activity is staying hidden

item.title: CISA Adds Six Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added six new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-21510 Microsoft Windows Shell Protection Mechanism Failure Vulnerability...

item.title: Poland Energy Sector Cyber Incident Highlights OT and ICS Security Gaps
item.description: The purpose of this Alert is to amplify Poland’s Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT Polska’s) Energy Sector Incident Report published on Jan. 30, 2026, and highlight key mitigations for Energy Sector stakeholders....

item.title: NCSC Issues Warning Over “Severe” Cyber-Attacks Targeting Critical National Infrastructure
item.description: NCSC call firms to ‘act now’ following disruptive malware attacks targeting Polish energy providers

item.title: European Governments Breached in Zero-Day Attacks Targeting Ivanti
item.description: The European Commission and government agencies in Finland and the Netherlands have suffered potentially related breaches

item.title: Inside the Human-AI Feedback Loop Powering CrowdStrike’s Agentic Security
February 2026 Patch Tuesday: Six Zero-Days Among 59 CVEs Patched
New Zero-Click Flaw in Claude Desktop Extensions, Anthropic Declines Fix
item.description: Security researchers from LayerX identified a new flaw in 50 Claude Desktop Extensions that could lead to unauthorized remote code execution

item.title: Two Connecticut Men Charged In Alleged $3m Gambling Fraud Scheme
item.description: Two Connecticut men face federal charges for a $3m scheme targeting online gambling platforms

item.title: VoidLink Malware Exhibits Multi-Cloud Capabilities and AI Code
item.description: VoidLink, a Linux-based C2 framework, facilitates credential theft, data exfiltration across clouds

item.description: High Vulnerabilities Primary Vendor -- Product Description Published CVSS Score Source Info Patch Info Insaat--Fikir Odalari AdminPando A SQL injection vulnerability exists in the login functionality of Fikir Odalari...

item.title: BridgePay Confirms Ransomware Attack, No Card Data Compromised
item.description: The services of Florida-based payments platform BridgePay are offline due to a ransomware attack

item.title: Social Media Platforms Earn Billions from Scam Ads
item.description: Revolut claims social media sites make £3.8bn annually from scam ads targeting European users

item.title: Researchers Find 40,000+ Exposed OpenClaw Instances
item.description: SecurityScorecard has identified over 40,000 OpenClaw deployments exposed to potential attack

item.title: US Agencies Told to Scrap End of Support Edge Devices
item.description: CISA has issued a new directive requiring federal agencies to decommission all end of support edge devices within 12 months to reduce ongoing exploitation risks

item.title: Novel Technique to Detect Cloud Threat Actor Operations
item.description: We introduce a novel method that maps cloud alert trends to MITRE ATT&CK techniques. The patterns created could identify threat actors by behavior. The post Novel Technique to Detect Cloud Threat Actor Operations appe...

item.title: Chinese-Made Malware Kit Targets Chinese-Based Routers and Edge Devices
item.description: DKnife is a Chinese made malware framework that targets Chinese-based users

item.title: Substack Confirms Data Breach, "Limited User Data" Compromised
item.description: Substack did not specify the number of users affected by the data breach

item.description: Infosecurity Europe 2026 will debut a new Cyber Startup Programme, featuring a dedicated show-floor zone for early-stage cybersecurity companies to showcase innovations, connect with investors and highlight emerging t...

item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2025-11953 React Native Community CLI OS Command Injection Vulnerability CVE-...

item.title: New Hacking Campaign Exploits Microsoft Windows WinRAR Vulnerability
item.description: Researchers at Check Point link ‘Amarath-Dragon’ attacks to prolific Chinese cyber-espionage operation

item.title: AI-Enabled Voice and Virtual Meeting Fraud Surges 1000%+
item.description: Pindrop warns of 1210% increase in AI-powered fraud last year

item.title: Global SystemBC Botnet Found Active Across 10,000 Infected Systems
item.description: SystemBC malware linked to 10,000 infected IPs, posing risks to sensitive government infrastructure

item.title: New Technical Markers Reveal Expanding ShadowSyndicate Cybercriminal Infrastructure
item.description: ShadowSyndicate cluster expands with new SSH fingerprints connecting servers to other ransomware ops

item.title: AI Drives Doubling of Phishing Attacks in a Year
item.description: Cofense claims AI is making phishing emails more personalized and sophisticated

item.title: Two Critical Flaws in n8n AI Workflow Automation Platform Allow Complete Takeover
item.description: Pillar Security discovered two new critical vulnerabilities in n8n that could lead to supply chain compromise, credential harvesting and complete takeover attacks

item.title: SolarWinds Web Help Desk Vulnerability Actively Exploited
item.description: CISA has added a critical CVE in SolarWinds Web Help Desk to its KEV Catalog

item.title: Why Smart People Fall For Phishing Attacks
item.description: Why do successful phishing attacks target our psychology rather than just our software? Discover Unit 42’s latest insights on defeating social engineering and securing your digital life. The post Why Smart People Fall...

item.title: Hundreds of Malicious Crypto Trading Add-Ons Found in Moltbot/OpenClaw
item.description: A security researcher found 386 malicious ‘skills’ published on ClawHub, a skill repository for the popular OpenClaw AI assistant project

item.title: DockerDash Exposes AI Supply Chain Weakness In Docker's Ask Gordon
item.description: DockerDash vulnerability allows RCE and data exfiltration via unverified metadata in Ask Gordon

item.title: UK ICO Launches Investigation into X Over AI Generated Non-Consensual Sexual Imagery
item.description: UK Data Protection Watchdog has “serious concerns” over data privacy on Elon Musk’s social platform

item.title: Researchers Warn of New “Vect” RaaS Variant
item.description: A new ransomware-as-a-service operation dubbed “Vect” features custom malware
